name: mobile-ios-design
description: Master iOS Human Interface Guidelines and SwiftUI patterns for building native iOS apps. Use when designing iOS interfaces, implementing SwiftUI views, or ensuring apps follow Apple's design principles.
capabilities: Provide iOS Human Interface Guidelines principles for clarity, deference, and depth
Generate SwiftUI code for navigation patterns including NavigationStack, TabView, and NavigationSplitView
Create adaptive layouts for iPhone and iPad using size classes and grid systems
Implement SF Symbols, semantic colors, and Dynamic Type for platform-native appearance
Design accessible interfaces with VoiceOver support and accessibility modifiers
Build toolbar patterns, search integration, and haptic feedback implementations
author: wshobson
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing iOS design guidelines and SwiftUI code examples. All static findings are false positives: markdown code blocks (```swift) were misidentified as shell backticks, Swift code patterns triggered crypto/recon heuristics, and Apple documentation URLs were flagged as hardcoded URLs. No executable code, network calls, credential handling, or shell commands present.
valueStatement: Building iOS apps requires following Apple Human Interface Guidelines and modern SwiftUI patterns. This skill provides comprehensive references for creating polished, accessible, and platform-native iOS interfaces.
limitations: Does not generate complete Xcode projects or iOS app scaffolding
Does not provide Swift coding beyond UI and design patterns
Does not integrate with Apple APIs or services beyond documentation references
Does not validate design decisions against app store review guidelines

useCases.title: Build iOS App Interfaces
useCases.description: Generate SwiftUI code for creating iOS app interfaces that follow Apple Human Interface Guidelines, including navigation, layouts, and system integration.

useCases.targetUser: Developers maintaining or upgrading iOS apps
useCases.title: Refactor Existing UI Code
useCases.description: Modernize legacy UIKit code to SwiftUI, or improve existing SwiftUI implementations to better follow platform conventions.

useCases.targetUser: Developers new to iOS or SwiftUI
useCases.title: Learn iOS Design Patterns
useCases.description: Study reference examples for common iOS design patterns including navigation, lists, forms, accessibility, and visual feedback.

promptTemplates.title: Create Navigation Pattern
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a SwiftUI navigation pattern using NavigationStack for an iOS app that shows a list of items. Include programmatic navigation and state persistence.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building a navigation structure for an iOS app

promptTemplates.title: Design Adaptive Layout
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an adaptive SwiftUI layout that uses size classes to show a single-column grid on iPhone and a three-column grid on iPad. Include safe area handling.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building responsive layouts for multiple iOS device sizes

promptTemplates.title: Implement Accessible Components
promptTemplates.prompt: Create accessible SwiftUI components including VoiceOver support, Dynamic Type scaling, and semantic color usage for automatic dark mode.

promptTemplates.title: Build Form with Validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a settings form in SwiftUI with text fields, toggles, pickers, and custom validation. Include @AppStorage for persistence and proper iOS form styling.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building user preference screens in iOS apps

bestPractices: Use semantic colors (.primary, .secondary, .background) instead of hardcoded colors for automatic light/dark mode support
Apply semantic fonts (.body, .headline, .title) instead of fixed sizes to support Dynamic Type
Include accessibility modifiers (.accessibilityLabel, .accessibilityHint) on all interactive elements
antiPatterns: Using fixed frame sizes or hardcoded padding values instead of flexible layouts and safe area insets
Ignoring size classes when designing for both iPhone and iPad form factors
Skipping accessibility testing or using fixed text instead of semantic fonts

faq.question: What iOS versions does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on modern iOS patterns (iOS 16+) including NavigationStack, but also includes legacy patterns like NavigationLink for compatibility with earlier versions.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete app projects?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides design patterns, component examples, and guidelines. You will need to integrate the generated code into your own Xcode project.

faq.question: How do I handle deep linking in iOS apps?
faq.answer: Use NavigationStack with NavigationPath to build deep linking support. Append destinations programmatically and handle URL schemes in your app delegate.

faq.question: What accessibility features should I implement?
faq.answer: Include .accessibilityLabel(), .accessibilityHint(), .accessibilityValue() on interactive views. Support Dynamic Type with semantic fonts and test with VoiceOver enabled.

faq.question: How do I support both iPhone and iPad layouts?
faq.answer: Use @Environment(\.horizontalSizeClass) to detect compact vs regular size classes. Create different column counts and layouts for each class using GridItem with .adaptive or .flexible.

faq.question: Does this skill cover UIKit or only SwiftUI?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on SwiftUI patterns. For UIKit patterns, you would need a separate skill or reference Apple's UIKit documentation.
